findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 34. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The North Carolina Marine Fisheries seal/logo with the '200 Years' celebration emblem is missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a circular seal/logo in the upper left corner with 'North Carolina Marine Fisheries' and a '200 Years' celebration badge. The HTML rendering does not display this image.

findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted between page-equivalent sections disrupts reading order in HTML.
findings.evidence: The HTML rendering shows the footer content (State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries, 3441 Arendell Street...) and then the header content (ROY COOPER Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER Secretary, KATHY B. RAWLS Director, FF-52-2023) repeated between the first and second page content, and again between pages 3 and 4. This creates reading order disruption as a reader encounters administrative header/footer blocks mid-document.

findings.description: The signature image of Kathy B. Rawls is missing from the HTML rendering on the final page.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 shows a handwritten signature of 'Kathy B. Rawls' above the typed name. The HTML rendering on the final page shows 'By:' with a blank line but no signature image.

findings.description: The footer text from the PDF pages (State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries, 3441 Arendell Street | P.O. Box 769 | Morehead City, North Carolina 28557, 252-726-7021) is not consolidated but rather absent from the HTML body. However, embedded images with page-level content appear mid-flow (between list items III.B and IV, and between items J and K), breaking the reading flow of the document.
findings.evidence: Two
